Container.Name: reclaimspace
Container.Overview:
Find Plex movies and TV episodes with duplicate files on disk. Reclaimspace keeps the file Radarr or Sonarr manages and **quarantines** the rest (never deleted).
**Web UI** on port 8777: setup wizard, dry-run scans, sortable/filterable reports, quarantine with manifests, restore preview.
Container.Description:
Reclaimspace finds duplicate Plex movies and TV episodes, keeps the file Radarr or Sonarr manages, and **quarantines** the rest. Nothing is deleted—files move to a timestamped folder with a rollback manifest.
**First run** 1. Open the Web UI at port **8777** (link on the Docker tab). 2. Complete the **setup wizard**, or open **Configuration** at `/config` if you already have settings. 3. Confirm container paths: Movies `/media/movies`, TV `/media/tv`, Quarantine `/quarantine` (must match your volume mappings). 4. Run a **movies dry run**, review the sortable report grid, then **quarantine** when ready.
**Requirements:** Plex plus Radarr (movies) and/or Sonarr (TV) on your network. The UI has no built-in login—use on a trusted LAN or behind an authenticated reverse proxy.
**Support:** [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/romwil/reclaimspace/issues)
**Documentation:** [README](https://github.com/romwil/reclaimspace) · [Docker / Unraid guide](https://github.com/romwil/reclaimspace/blob/main/docs/DOCKER.md) · [Web UI](https://github.com/romwil/reclaimspace/blob/main/docs/WEB_UI.md)

Config.Name: Movies library
Config.Description: Host movie library (set Movies root to /media/movies in Configuration)

Config.Name: TV library
Config.Description: Host TV library (set TV root to /media/tv in Configuration)

Config.Name: PLEX_URL
Config.Description: Optional seed: Plex URL (also editable in web UI)
